mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals economic deceleration, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and deceleration in auto sales indicates demand destruction under inflationary pressure, forcing equity markets to discount future cash flows and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over the 2010-2024 sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive industrial production surprises are negative in more than 60 percent of cases, or predictive R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and yields positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all rolling 5-year windows, or signal accuracy falls below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.4 million units, it signals strong construction sector demand and wealth effects that boost consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 58 percent of triggered observations, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate indicates a loss of central bank credibility, triggering equity sell-offs as real discount rates remain elevated and forward returns turn negative.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests, or 20-day returns remain positive during high-inflation low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP during an early-cycle expansion phase confirms broad-based economic momentum, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDP revisions are negative in more than 65 percent of cases, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive information coefficient over any 4-year window.
